title: Represent linear systems with matrices
description: Determine the matrix that represents a given system of linear equations.
topic_path: Topic C: Systems of linear equations

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}2x-y+2z+t&=-2
\\x+y-5t&=5\end{align}$

**Which of the following matrices represents this system?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$
###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}2x-y+2z+t&=-2
\\x+y-5t&=5\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{2}x+(\blueD{-1})y+\blueD{2}z+\blueD{1}t&=\blueD{-2}
\\\blueD{1}x+\blueD{1}y+\blueD{0}z+(\blueD{-5})t&=\blueD{5}\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
2 & -1 & 2 & 1 & -2 \\
1 & 1 & 0 & -5 & 5 \end{array} \right]$ 
###Summary

This is the correct augmented matrix. 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
2 & -1 & 2 & 1 & -2 \\
1 & 1 & 0 & -5 & 5 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}3x+4y-2t&=-11
\\8x-9y-2z+5t&=18\end{align}$

**Which of the following matrices represents this system?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$
###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}3x+4y-2t&=-11
\\8x-9y-2z+5t&=18\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{3}x+\blueD{4}y+\blueD{0}z+(\blueD{-2})t&=\blueD{-11}
\\\blueD{8}x+(\blueD{-9})y+(\blueD{-2})z+\blueD{5}t&=\blueD{18}\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
3 & 4 & 0 & -2 & -11 \\
8 & -9 & -2 & 5 & 18 \end{array} \right]$ 
###Summary

This is the correct augmented matrix. 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
3 & 4 & 0 & -2 & -11 \\
8 & -9 & -2 & 5 & 18 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}x+4y+3z+t&=0
\\7x-5z+7t&=4\end{align}$

**Which of the following matrices represents this system?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}x+4y+3z+t&=0
\\7x-5z+7t&=4\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{1}x+\blueD{4}y+\blueD{3}z+\blueD{1}t&=\blueD{0}
\\\blueD{7}x+\blueD{0}y+(\blueD{-5})z+\blueD{7}t&=\blueD{4}\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
1 & 4 & 3 & 1 & 0 \\
7 & 0 & -5 & 7 & 4 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

This is the correct augmented matrix. 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
1 & 4 & 3 & 1 & 0 \\
7 & 0 & -5 & 7 & 4 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}6x+5y-7z+8t&=9
\\y-6z+t&=-1\end{align}$

**Which of the following matrices represents this system?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}6x+5y-7z+8t&=9
\\y-6z+t&=-1\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{6}x+\blueD{5}y+(\blueD{-7})z+\blueD{8}t&=\blueD{9}
\\\blueD{0}x+\blueD{1}y+(\blueD{-6})z+\blueD{1}t&=\blueD{-1}\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
6 & 5 & -7 & 8 & 9 \\
0 & 1 & -6 & 1 & -1 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

This is the correct augmented matrix. 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
6 & 5 & -7 & 8 & 9 \\
0 & 1 & -6 & 1 & -1 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}10x+3y+5z-t&=10
\\11x-y&=4\end{align}$

**Which of the following matrices represents this system?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$
###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}10x+3y+5z-t&=10
\\11x-y&=4\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{10}x+\blueD{3}y+\blueD{5}z+(\blueD{-1})t&=\blueD{10}
\\\blueD{11}x+(\blueD{-1})y+\blueD{0}z+\blueD{0}t&=\blueD{4}\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
10 & 3 & 5 & -1 & 10 \\
11 & -1 & 0 & 0 & 4 \end{array} \right]$ 
###Summary

This is the correct augmented matrix. 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
10 & 3 & 5 & -1 & 10 \\
11 & -1 & 0 & 0 & 4 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}3x+2y+z-7t&=1
\\9x-15z-8t&=13\end{align}$

**Which of the following matrices represents this system?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}3x+2y+z-7t&=1
\\9x-15z-8t&=13\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{3}x+\blueD{2}y+\blueD{1}z+(\blueD{-7})t&=\blueD{1}
\\\blueD{9}x+\blueD{0}y+(\blueD{-15})z+(\blueD{-8})t&=\blueD{13}\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
3 & 2 & 1 & -7 & 1 \\
9 & 0 & -15 & -8 & 13 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

This is the correct augmented matrix. 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
3 & 2 & 1 & -7 & 1 \\
9 & 0 & -15 & -8 & 13  \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}4x+3y+3z-3t&=6
\\7y+2z-12t&=9\end{align}$

**Which of the following matrices represents this system?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}4x+3y+3z-3t&=6
\\7y+2z-12t&=9\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{4}x+\blueD{3}y+\blueD{3}z+(\blueD{-3})t&=\blueD{6}
\\\blueD{0}x+\blueD{7}y+\blueD{2}z+(\blueD{-12})t&=\blueD{9}\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
4 & 3 & 3 & -3 & 6 \\
0 & 7 & 2 & -12 & 9 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

This is the correct augmented matrix. 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
4 & 3 & 3 & -3 & 6 \\
0 & 7 & 2 & -12 & 9 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}9x+5z-t&=10
\\12x+3y+2z-10t&=4\end{align}$

**Which of the following matrices represents this system?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}9x+5z-t&=10
\\12x+3y+2z-10t&=4\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{9}x+\blueD{0}y+\blueD{5}z+(\blueD{-1})t&=\blueD{10}
\\\blueD{12}x+\blueD{3}y+\blueD{2}z+(\blueD{-10})t&=\blueD{4}\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
9 & 0 & 5 & -1 & 10 \\
12 & 3 & 2 & -10 & 4 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

This is the correct augmented matrix. 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
9 & 0 & 5 & -1 & 10 \\
12 & 3 & 2 & -10 & 4 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}4x+4y-3z-t&=12
\\-10x-y+3t&=14\end{align}$

**Which of the following matrices represents this system?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$
###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}4x+4y-3z-t&=12
\\-10x-y+3t&=14\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{4}x+\blueD{4}y+(\blueD{-3})z+(\blueD{-1})t&=\blueD{12}
\\(\blueD{-10})x+(\blueD{-1})y+\blueD{0}z+\blueD{3}t&=\blueD{14}\end{align}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
4 & 4 & -3 & -1 & 12 \\
-10 & -1 & 0 & 3 & 14 \end{array} \right]$ 
###Summary

This is the correct augmented matrix. 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
4 & 4 & -3 & -1 & 12 \\
-10 & -1 & 0 & 3 & 14 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}12x+2y+z+4t&=9
\\5x+15y-10z+20t&=-5\end{align}$

**Which of the following matrices represents this system?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}12x+2y+z+4t&=9
\\5x+15y-10z+20t&=-5\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{12}x+\blueD{2}y+\blueD{1}z+\blueD{4}t&=\blueD{9}
\\\blueD{5}x+\blueD{15}y+(\blueD{-10})z+\blueD{20}t&=\blueD{-5}\end{align}$

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
12 & 2 & 1 & 4 & 9 \\
5 & 15 & -10 & 20 & -5 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

This is the correct augmented matrix. 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
12 & 2 & 1 & 4 & 9 \\
5 & 15 & -10 & 20 & -5 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write the following system of equations as an augmented matrix.**  
*Represent each row and column in the order in which the variables and equations appear below.* 


>$\begin{align}3x+y=1
\\2x-2y=0\end{align}$

[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}3x+y=1
\\2x-2y=0\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{3}x+\blueD{1}y&=\blueD{1}
\\\blueD{2}x+(\blueD{-2})y&=\blueD{0}\end{align}$

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.


>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
3 & 1 & 1 \\
2 & -2 & 0 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

Our augmented matrix is: 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
3 & 1 & 1 \\
2 & -2 & 0 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write the following system of equations as an augmented matrix.**  
*Represent each row and column in the order in which the variables and equations appear below.* 


>$\begin{align}2x-3y=-1
\\5x+2y=7\end{align}$

[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}2x-3y=-1
\\5x+2y=7\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{2}x+(\blueD{-3})y&=\blueD{-1}
\\\blueD{5}x+\blueD{2}y&=\blueD{7}\end{align}$

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.


>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
2 & -3 & -1 \\
5 & 2 & 7 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

Our augmented matrix is: 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
2 & -3 & -1 \\
5 & 2 & 7 \end{array} \right]$

[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

>$\begin{align}4x+7y=15
\\6x-8y=4\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{4}x+\blueD{7}y&=\blueD{15}
\\\blueD{6}x+(\blueD{-8})y&=\blueD{4}\end{align}$

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.


>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
4 & 7 & 15 \\
6 & -8 & 4 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

Our augmented matrix is:

4 & 7 & 15 \\
6 & -8 & 4 \end{array} \right]$

[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
###Background

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.


>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}

8 & -5 & 19 \\

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write the following system of equations as an augmented matrix.**  
*Represent each row and column in the order in which the variables and equations appear below.* 


>$\begin{align}-5x+5y=-15
\\9x+10y=46\end{align}$

[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}-5x+5y=-15
\\9x+10y=46\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{-5}x+\blueD{5}y&=\blueD{-15}
\\\blueD{9}x+\blueD{10}y&=\blueD{46}\end{align}$

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.


>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
-5 & 5 & -15 \\
9 & 10 & 46 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

Our augmented matrix is: 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
-5 & 5 & -15 \\
9 & 10 & 46 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write the following system of equations as an augmented matrix.**  
*Represent each row and column in the order in which the variables and equations appear below.* 


>$\begin{align}10x-15y=5
\\3x+2y=8\end{align}$

[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}10x-15y=5
\\3x+2y=8\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{10}x+(\blueD{-15})y&=\blueD{5}
\\\blueD{3}x+\blueD{2}y&=\blueD{8}\end{align}$

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.


>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
10 & -15 & 5 \\
3 & 2 & 8 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

Our augmented matrix is: 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
10 & -15 & 5 \\
3 & 2 & 8\end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write the following system of equations as an augmented matrix.**  
*Represent each row and column in the order in which the variables and equations appear below.* 


>$\begin{align}6x+9y=3
\\-3x+5y=-11\end{align}$

[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}6x+9y=3
\\-3x+5y=-11\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{6}x+\blueD{9}y&=\blueD{3}
\\\blueD{-3}x+\blueD{5}y&=\blueD{-11}\end{align}$

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.


>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
6 & 9 & 3 \\
-3 & 5 & -11 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

Our augmented matrix is: 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
6 & 9 & 3 \\
-3 & 5 & -11 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write the following system of equations as an augmented matrix.**  
*Represent each row and column in the order in which the variables and equations appear below.* 


>$\begin{align}4x+4y=-8
\\9x+y=6\end{align}$

[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}4x+4y=-8
\\9x+y=6\end{align}$


First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{4}x+\blueD{4}y&=\blueD{-8}
\\\blueD{9}x+\blueD{1}y&=\blueD{6}\end{align}$

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.


>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
4 & 4 & -8 \\
9 & 1 & 6 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

Our augmented matrix is: 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
4 & 4 & -8 \\
9 & 1 & 6 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write the following system of equations as an augmented matrix.**  
*Represent each row and column in the order in which the variables and equations appear below.* 


>$\begin{align}2x+12y=-14
\\3x-y=2\end{align}$

[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}2x+12y=-14
\\3x-y=2\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{2}x+\blueD{12}y&=\blueD{-14}
\\\blueD{3}x+(\blueD{-1})y&=\blueD{2}\end{align}$

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.


>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
2 & 12 & -14 \\
3 & -1 & 2 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

Our augmented matrix is: 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
2 & 12 & -14 \\
3 & -1 & 2 \end{array} \right]$

assessment_items.question_content:
**Write the following system of equations as an augmented matrix.**  
*Represent each row and column in the order in which the variables and equations appear below.* 


>$\begin{align}x+5y=24
\\x-8y=-41\end{align}$

[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
###Background 

We can express a system of equations in  an *augmented matrix* in which each row represents one equation. To do this, we write the coefficients of each variable in its own column, and the constants on the right side of the equations in the last column.

For example, if we are given a system of equations in two variables, then we can express this as an augmented matrix as follows. 

>$\begin{align}ax+by&=m
\\cx+dy&=n\end{align}$  $\qquad \rightarrow$ $\qquad \left[\begin{array} {ccc}
a & b & m \\
c & d & n \end{array} \right]$

###Representing the system of equations as a matrix

We are given the system of equations: 

>$\begin{align}x+5y=24
\\x-8y=-41\end{align}$

First, let's rewrite this system to show the coefficients of each variable. 

>$\begin{align}\blueD{1}x+\blueD{5}y&=\blueD{24}
\\\blueD{1}x+(\blueD{-8})y&=\blueD{-41}\end{align}$

Now, we can write our augmented matrix.


>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
1 & 5 & 24 \\
1 & -8 & -41 \end{array} \right]$

###Summary

Our augmented matrix is: 

>$\left[\begin{array} {ccc}
1 & 5 & 24 \\
1 & -8 & -41 \end{array} \right]$
